verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ cum audisset Ezechias rex, scidit vestimenta sua, et opertus est sacco, ingressusque est domum Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י כִּשְׁמֹ֙עַ֙ הַמֶּ֣לֶךְ חִזְקִיָּ֔הוּ וַיִּקְרַ֖ע אֶת־בְּגָדָ֑יו וַיִּתְכַּ֣ס בַּשָּׂ֔ק וַיָּבֹ֖א בֵּ֥ית יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when king Ezechias heard these words, he rent his garments, and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin kuningas Hiskia sen kuuli, repäisi hän vaatteensa ja puki säkin yllensä ja meni Herran huoneesen.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when king Hezekiah heard it, that he rent his clothes, and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et misit Eliacim præpositum domus, et Sobnam scribam, et senes de sacerdotibus, opertos saccis, ad Isaiam prophetam filium Amos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֠יִּשְׁלַ֠ח אֶת־אֶלְיָקִ֨ים אֲשֶׁר־עַל־הַבַּ֜יִת וְשֶׁבְנָ֣א הַסֹּפֵ֗ר וְאֵת֙ זִקְנֵ֣י הַכֹּהֲנִ֔ים מִתְכַּסִּ֖ים בַּשַּׂקִּ֑ים אֶל־יְשַׁעְיָ֥הוּ הַנָּבִ֖יא בֶּן־אָמֽוֹץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he sent Eliacim, who was over the house, and Sobna, the scribe, and the ancients of the priests, covered with sackcloths, to Isaias, the prophet, the son of Amos.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän lähetti Eliakimin huoneenhaltian ja Sebnan kirjoittajan vanhimpain pappien kanssa, puetettuina säkeillä, prophetan Jesaian Amotsin pojan tykö.
verses.king_james: And he sent Eliakim, which was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth, to Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixerunt : Hæc dicit Ezechias : Dies tribulationis, et increpationis, et blasphemiæ dies iste : venerunt filii usque ad partum, et vires non habet parturiens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלָ֗יו כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר חִזְקִיָּ֔הוּ יוֹם־צָרָ֧ה וְתוֹכֵחָ֛ה וּנְאָצָ֖ה הַיּ֣וֹם הַזֶּ֑ה כִּ֣י בָ֤אוּ בָנִים֙ עַד־מַשְׁבֵּ֔ר וְכֹ֥חַ אַ֖יִן לְלֵדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they said to him: Thus saith Ezechias: This day is a day of tribulation, and of rebuke, and of blasphemy: the children are come to the birth, and the woman in travail hath not strength.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he sanoivat hänelle: näin sanoo Hiskia: tämä on vaivan, toran ja häväistyksen päivä; sillä lapset ovat tulleet synnyttämiselle, mutta ei ole voimaa synnyttää.
verses.king_james: And they said unto him, Thus saith Hezekiah, This day is a day of trouble, and of rebuke, and blasphemy: for the children are come to the birth, and there is not strength to bring forth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si forte audiat Dominus Deus tuus universa verba Rabsacis, quem misit rex Assyriorum dominus suus ut exprobraret Deum viventem et argueret verbis, quæ audivit Dominus Deus tuus : et fac orationem pro reliquiis quæ repertæ sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוּלַ֡י יִשְׁמַע֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהֶ֜יךָ אֵ֣ת ׀ כׇּל־דִּבְרֵ֣י רַבְשָׁקֵ֗ה אֲשֶׁר֩ שְׁלָח֨וֹ מֶֽלֶךְ־אַשּׁ֤וּר ׀ אֲדֹנָיו֙ לְחָרֵף֙ אֱלֹהִ֣ים חַ֔י וְהוֹכִ֙יחַ֙ בַּדְּבָרִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁמַ֖ע יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֶ֑יךָ וְנָשָׂ֣אתָ תְפִלָּ֔ה בְּעַ֥ד הַשְּׁאֵרִ֖ית הַנִּמְצָאָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: It may be the Lord, thy God, will hear all the words of Rabsaces, whom the king of the Assyrians, his master, hath sent to reproach the living God, and to reprove with words, which the Lord, thy God, hath heard: and do thou offer prayer for the remnants that are found.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jospa Herra sinun Jumalas kuulis kaikki Rabsaken sanat, jonka hänen herransa Assyrian kuningas on lähettänyt tänne pilkkaamaan elävää Jumalaa, että hän nuhtelis häntä niiden sanain tähden, jotka Herra sinun Jumalas kuullut on: niin korota sinun rukoukses niiden edestä, jotka vielä jääneet ovat;
verses.king_james: It may be the Lord thy God will hear all the words of Rab–shakeh, whom the king of Assyria his master hath sent to reproach the living God; and will reprove the words which the Lord thy God hath heard: wherefore lift up thy prayer for the remnant that are left.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt ergo servi regis Ezechiæ ad Isaiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֗אוּ עַבְדֵ֛י הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ אֶֽל־יְשַׁעְיָֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the servants of king Ezechias came to Isaias.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin kuningas Hiskian palveliat tulivat Jesaian tykö,
verses.king_james: So the servants of king Hezekiah came to Isaiah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque eis Isaias : Hæc dicetis domino vestro : Hæc dicit Dominus : Noli timere a facie sermonum quos audisti, quibus blasphemaverunt pueri regis Assyriorum me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָהֶם֙ יְשַֽׁעְיָ֔הוּ כֹּ֥ה תֹאמְר֖וּן אֶל־אֲדֹנֵיכֶ֑ם כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה אַל־תִּירָא֙ מִפְּנֵ֤י הַדְּבָרִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר שָׁמַ֔עְתָּ אֲשֶׁ֧ר גִּדְּפ֛וּ נַעֲרֵ֥י מֶלֶךְ־אַשּׁ֖וּר אֹתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias said to them: Thus shall you say to your master: Thus saith the Lord: Be not afraid for the words which thou hast heard, with which the servants of the king of the Assyrians have blasphemed me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sanoi Jesaia heille: näin sanokaat teidän herrallenne: näin sanoo Herra: älä mitään peljästy niiden sanain tähden, jotka sinä kuullut olet, joilla Assyrian kuninkaan palveliat minua häväisseet ovat.
verses.king_james: And Isaiah said unto them, Thus shall ye say to your master, Thus saith the Lord, Be not afraid of the words which thou hast heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego immittam ei spiritum, et audiet nuntium, et revertetur in terram suam, et dejiciam eum gladio in terra sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְנִ֨י נֹתֵ֥ן בּוֹ֙ ר֔וּחַ וְשָׁמַ֥ע שְׁמוּעָ֖ה וְשָׁ֣ב לְאַרְצ֑וֹ וְהִפַּלְתִּ֥יו בַּחֶ֖רֶב בְּאַרְצֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold I will send a spirit upon him, and he shall hear a message, and shall return into his own country, and I will make him fall by the sword in his own country.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, minä annan hänelle hengen, että hän saa kuulla sanoman, ja palajaa omalle maallensa; ja minä annan hänen kaatua miekalla omassa maakunnassansa.
verses.king_james: Behold, I will send a blast upon him, and he shall hear a rumour, and shall return to his own land; and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversus est ergo Rabsaces, et invenit regem Assyriorum expugnantem Lobnam : audierat enim quod recessisset de Lachis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֙שׇׁב֙ רַבְשָׁקֵ֔ה וַיִּמְצָא֙ אֶת־מֶ֣לֶךְ אַשּׁ֔וּר נִלְחָ֖ם עַל־לִבְנָ֑ה כִּ֣י שָׁמַ֔ע כִּ֥י נָסַ֖ע מִלָּכִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Rabsaces returned, and found the king of the Assyrians besieging Lobna: for he had heard that he was departed from Lachis.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Rabsake palasi, löysi hän Assyrian kuninkaan sotimasta Libnaa vastaan; sillä hän oli kuullut hänen menneeksi Lakiksesta pois.
verses.king_james: So Rab–shakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria warring against Libnah: for he had heard that he was departed from Lachish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque audisset de Tharaca rege Æthiopiæ, dicentes : Ecce egressus est ut pugnet adversum te : et iret contra eum, misit nuntios ad Ezechiam, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֗ע אֶל־תִּרְהָ֤קָה מֶֽלֶךְ־כּוּשׁ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הִנֵּ֥ה יָצָ֖א לְהִלָּחֵ֣ם אִתָּ֑ךְ וַיָּ֙שׇׁב֙ וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח מַלְאָכִ֔ים אֶל־חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he heard of Tharaca, king of Ethiopia: Behold, he is come out to fight with thee: and was going against him, he sent messengers to Ezechias, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän sai kuulla Tirhakasta Ethiopian kuninkaasta sanottavan: katso, hän on mennyt sotimaan sinua vastaan; niin hän palasi, ja lähetti sanan Hiskialle ja antoi hänelle sanoa:
verses.king_james: And when he heard say of Tirhakah king of Ethiopia, Behold, he is come out to fight against thee: he sent messengers again unto Hezekiah, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicite Ezechiæ regi Juda : Non te seducat Deus tuus in quo habes fiduciam, neque dicas : Non tradetur Jerusalem in manus regis Assyriorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה תֹאמְר֗וּן אֶל־חִזְקִיָּ֤הוּ מֶֽלֶךְ־יְהוּדָה֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר אַל־יַשִּׁאֲךָ֣ אֱלֹהֶ֔יךָ אֲשֶׁ֥ר אַתָּ֛ה בֹּטֵ֥חַ בּ֖וֹ לֵאמֹ֑ר לֹ֤א תִנָּתֵן֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם בְּיַ֖ד מֶ֥לֶךְ אַשּֽׁוּר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thus shall you say to Ezechias, king of Juda: Let not thy God deceive thee, in whom thou trustest: and do not say: Jerusalem shall not be delivered into the hands of the king of the Assyrians.
verses.finnish_biblia: Puhukaat näin Hiskialle Juudan kuninkaalle, sanoen: älä anna Jumalas, jonka päälle sinä luotat, vietellä sinuas ja sanot: ei Jerusalem anneta Assyrian kuninkaan käteen.
verses.king_james: Thus shall ye speak to Hezekiah king of Judah, saying, Let not thy God in whom thou trustest deceive thee, saying, Jerusalem shall not be delivered into the hand of the king of Assyria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu enim ipse audisti quæ fecerunt reges Assyriorum universis terris, quomodo vastaverunt eas : num ergo solus poteris liberari ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֣ה ׀ אַתָּ֣ה שָׁמַ֗עְתָּ אֵת֩ אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָשׂ֜וּ מַלְכֵ֥י אַשּׁ֛וּר לְכׇל־הָאֲרָצ֖וֹת לְהַחֲרִימָ֑ם וְאַתָּ֖ה תִּנָּצֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold, thou hast heard what the kings of the Assyrians have done to all countries, how they have laid them waste: and canst thou alone be delivered?
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, sinä olet kuullut, mitä Assyrian kuninkaat tehneet ovat kaikille maakunnille, ja hukuttaneet heitä: ja sinä pelastettaisiin?
verses.king_james: Behold, thou hast heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, by destroying them utterly: and shalt thou be delivered?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid liberaverunt dii gentium singulos quos vastaverunt patres mei, Gozan videlicet, et Haran, et Reseph, et filios Eden qui erant in Thelassar ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַהִצִּ֨ילוּ אֹתָ֜ם אֱלֹהֵ֤י הַגּוֹיִם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר שִׁחֲת֣וּ אֲבוֹתַ֔י אֶת־גּוֹזָ֖ן וְאֶת־חָרָ֑ן וְרֶ֥צֶף וּבְנֵי־עֶ֖דֶן אֲשֶׁ֥ר בִּתְלַאשָּֽׂר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Have the gods of the nations delivered any of them, whom my fathers have destroyed, to wit, Gozan, and Haran, and Reseph, and the children of Eden, that were in Thelassar?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ovatko myös pakanain jumalat pelastaneet heidät, jotka minun isäni hukuttaneet ovat, Gosanin, Haranin, niin myös Resephin ja Edenin lapset, jotka Telassarissa olivat?
verses.king_james: Have the gods of the nations delivered them which my fathers have destroyed; as Gozan, and Haran, and Rezeph, and the children of Eden which were in Thelasar?

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi est rex Emath, et rex Arphad, et rex civitatis Sepharvaim, Ana, et Ava ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַיּ֤וֹ מֶֽלֶךְ־חֲמָת֙ וּמֶ֣לֶךְ אַרְפָּ֔ד וּמֶ֖לֶךְ לָעִ֣יר סְפַרְוָ֑יִם הֵנַ֖ע וְעִוָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Where is the king of Emath, and the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, and of Ana, and of Ava?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kussa on Hematin kuningas, Arpadin kuningas, Sepharvaimin, Henan ja Ivvan kaupungin kuningas?
verses.king_james: Where is the king of Hamath, and the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Hena, and Ivah?

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque cum accepisset Ezechias litteras de manu nuntiorum, et legisset eas, ascendit in domum Domini, et expandit eas coram Domino,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֨ח חִזְקִיָּ֧הוּ אֶת־הַסְּפָרִ֛ים מִיַּ֥ד הַמַּלְאָכִ֖ים וַיִּקְרָאֵ֑ם וַיַּ֙עַל֙ בֵּ֣ית יְהֹוָ֔ה וַיִּפְרְשֵׂ֥הוּ חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ לִפְנֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And when Ezechias had received the letter of the hand of the messengers, and had read it, he went up to the house of the Lord, and spread it before the Lord,
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Hiskia oli ottanut kirjan sanansaattajilta ja lukenut sen, meni hän Herran huoneesen, ja Hiskia levitti sen Herran eteen,
verses.king_james: And Hezekiah received the letter of the hand of the messengers, and read it: and Hezekiah went up into the house of the Lord, and spread it before the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: et oravit in conspectu ejus, dicens : Domine Deus Israël, qui sedes super cherubim, tu es Deus solus regum omnium terræ : tu fecisti cælum et terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתְפַּלֵּ֨ל חִזְקִיָּ֜הוּ לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָה֮ וַיֹּאמַר֒ יְהֹוָ֞ה אֱלֹהֵ֤י יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ יֹשֵׁ֣ב הַכְּרֻבִ֔ים אַתָּה־ה֤וּא הָאֱלֹהִים֙ לְבַדְּךָ֔ לְכֹ֖ל מַמְלְכ֣וֹת הָאָ֑רֶץ אַתָּ֣ה עָשִׂ֔יתָ אֶת־הַשָּׁמַ֖יִם וְאֶת־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he prayed in his sight, saying: O Lord God of Israel, who sittest upon the cherubims, thou alone art the God of all the kings of the earth: thou madest heaven and earth:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja rukoili Herran edessä ja sanoi: Herra Israelin Jumala, joka istut Kerubimin päällä, sinä olet ainoa Jumala kaikissa maan valtakunnissa, sinä olet tehnyt taivaan ja maan.
verses.king_james: And Hezekiah prayed before the Lord, and said, O Lord God of Israel, which dwellest between the cherubims, thou art the God, even thou alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth; thou hast made heaven and earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inclina aurem tuam, et audi : aperi, Domine, oculos tuos, et vide : audi omnia verba Sennacherib, qui misit ut exprobraret nobis Deum viventem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַטֵּ֨ה יְהֹוָ֤ה ׀ אׇזְנְךָ֙ וּֽשְׁמָ֔ע פְּקַ֧ח יְהֹוָ֛ה עֵינֶ֖יךָ וּרְאֵ֑ה וּשְׁמַ֗ע אֵ֚ת דִּבְרֵ֣י סַנְחֵרִ֔יב אֲשֶׁ֣ר שְׁלָח֔וֹ לְחָרֵ֖ף אֱלֹהִ֥ים חָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Incline thy ear, and hear: open, O Lord, thy eyes and see: and hear all the words of Sennacherib, who hath sent to upbraid unto us the living God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra, kallista korvas ja kuule, avaa Herra silmäs ja näe, ja kuule Sanheribin sanat, joka tänne on lähettänyt pilkkaamaan elävää Jumalaa!
verses.king_james: Lord, bow down thine ear, and hear: open, Lord, thine eyes, and see: and hear the words of Sennacherib, which hath sent him to reproach the living God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vere, Domine, dissipaverunt reges Assyriorum gentes, et terras omnium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇמְנָ֖ם יְהֹוָ֑ה הֶחֱרִ֜יבוּ מַלְכֵ֥י אַשּׁ֛וּר אֶת־הַגּוֹיִ֖ם וְאֶת־אַרְצָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Of a truth, O Lord, the kings of the Assyrians have destroyed nations, and the lands of them all.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se on tosi, Herra, että Assyrian kuninkaat ovat hävittäneet pakanat ja heidän maakuntansa,
verses.king_james: Of a truth, Lord, the kings of Assyria have destroyed the nations and their lands,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et miserunt deos eorum in ignem : non enim erant dii, sed opera manuum hominum, ex ligno et lapide : et perdiderunt eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָתְנ֥וּ אֶת־אֱלֹהֵיהֶ֖ם בָּאֵ֑שׁ כִּי֩ לֹ֨א אֱלֹהִ֜ים הֵ֗מָּה כִּ֣י אִם־מַעֲשֵׂ֧ה יְדֵי־אָדָ֛ם עֵ֥ץ וָאֶ֖בֶן וַֽיְאַבְּדֽוּם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they have cast their gods into the fire: for they were not gods, but the work of men's hands, of wood and stone, and they destroyed them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ovat heittäneet heidän jumalansa tuleen; sillä ei ne olleet jumalia, mutta ihmisten kätten teot, puut ja kivet, sentähden ovat he ne hävittäneet.
verses.king_james: And have cast their gods into the fire: for they were no gods, but the work of men’s hands, wood and stone: therefore they have destroyed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc igitur Domine Deus noster, salvos nos fac de manu ejus, ut sciant omnia regna terræ quia tu es Dominus Deus solus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּה֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵ֔ינוּ הוֹשִׁיעֵ֥נוּ נָ֖א מִיָּד֑וֹ וְיֵֽדְעוּ֙ כׇּל־מַמְלְכ֣וֹת הָאָ֔רֶץ כִּ֥י אַתָּ֛ה יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים לְבַדֶּֽךָ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore, O Lord our God, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that thou art the Lord, the only God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta nyt sinä, Herra meidän Jumalamme, auta meitä hänen kädestänsä: että kaikki maan valtakunnat tietäisivät sinun, Herra, olevan ainoan Jumalan.
verses.king_james: Now therefore, O Lord our God, I beseech thee, save thou us out of his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that thou art the Lord God, even thou only.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit autem Isaias filius Amos ad Ezechiam, dicens : Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël : Quæ deprecatus es me super Sennacherib rege Assyriorum, audivi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַח֙ יְשַׁעְיָ֣הוּ בֶן־אָמ֔וֹץ אֶל־חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ לֵאמֹ֑ר כֹּֽה־אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֨ר הִתְפַּלַּ֧לְתָּ אֵלַ֛י אֶל־סַנְחֵרִ֥ב מֶלֶךְ־אַשּׁ֖וּר שָׁמָֽעְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias, the son of Amos, sent to Ezechias, saying: Thus saith the Lord, the God of Israel: I have heard the prayer thou hast made to me concerning Sennacherib, king of the Assyrians.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin lähetti Jesaia Amotsin poika Hiskian tykö ja antoi hänelle sanoa: näin sanoo Herra Israelin Jumala: minä olen kuullut, mitäs minulta rukoillut olet Sanheribin Assyrian kuninkaan tähden.
verses.king_james: Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, That which thou hast prayed to me against Sennacherib king of Assyria I have heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iste est sermo, quem locutus est Dominus de eo :   Sprevit te, et subsannavit te, virgo filia Sion :  post tergum tuum caput movit, filia Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֣ה הַדָּבָ֔ר אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה עָלָ֑יו בָּזָ֨ה לְךָ֜ לָעֲגָ֣ה לְךָ֗ בְּתוּלַת֙ בַּת־צִיּ֔וֹן אַחֲרֶ֙יךָ֙ רֹ֣אשׁ הֵנִ֔יעָה בַּ֖ת יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: This is the word that the Lord hath spoken of him: The virgin, the daughter of Sion, hath despised thee, and laughed thee to scorn: the daughter of Jerusalem hath wagged her head behind thy back.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nämät ovat ne, mitkä Herra häntä vastaan puhunut on: neitsy Zionin tytär katsoo sinun ylön ja pilkkaa sinua, Jerusalemin tytär vääntelee päätänsä sinun jälkees.
verses.king_james: This is the word that the Lord hath spoken concerning him; The virgin the daughter of Zion hath despised thee, and laughed thee to scorn; the daughter of Jerusalem hath shaken her head at thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui exprobrasti, et quem blasphemasti ?  contra quem exaltasti vocem tuam, et elevasti in excelsum oculos tuos ?  Contra Sanctum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־מִ֤י חֵרַ֙פְתָּ֙ וְגִדַּ֔פְתָּ וְעַל־מִ֖י הֲרִימ֣וֹתָ קּ֑וֹל וַתִּשָּׂ֥א מָר֛וֹם עֵינֶ֖יךָ עַל־קְד֥וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whom hast thou reproached, and whom hast thou blasphemed? against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thy eyes on high? against the holy one of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ketäs olet pilkannut ja häväissyt? ketä vastaan sinä olet korottanut äänes? Sinä olet nostanut silmäs pyhää vastaan Israelissa.
verses.king_james: Whom hast thou reproached and blasphemed? and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thine eyes on high? even against the Holy One of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per manum servorum tuorum exprobrasti Domino, et dixisti :  In multitudine curruum meorum ascendi excelsa montium in summitate Libani,  et succidi sublimes cedros ejus, et electas abietes illius.  Et ingressus sum usque ad terminos ejus,  et saltum Carmeli ejus
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּיַ֣ד מַלְאָכֶ֘יךָ֮ חֵרַ֣פְתָּ ׀ אֲדֹנָי֒ וַתֹּ֗אמֶר (ברכב) [בְּרֹ֥ב] רִכְבִּ֛י אֲנִ֥י עָלִ֛יתִי מְר֥וֹם הָרִ֖ים יַרְכְּתֵ֣י לְבָנ֑וֹן וְאֶכְרֹ֞ת קוֹמַ֤ת אֲרָזָיו֙ מִבְח֣וֹר בְּרֹשָׁ֔יו וְאָב֙וֹאָה֙ מְל֣וֹן קִצֹּ֔ה יַ֖עַר כַּרְמִלּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: By the hand of thy servants thou hast reproached the Lord, and hast said: With the multitude of my chariots I have gone up to the height of the mountains, to the top of Libanus, and have cut down its tall cedars, and its choice fir trees. And I have entered into the furthest parts thereof, and the forest of its Carmel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet sanansaattajais kautta häväissyt Herraa ja sanonut: minä olen noussut vuoren kukkuloille vaunuini paljoudella, Libanonin sivulle: minä tahdon hakata maahan hänen korkiat sedripuunsa ja valitut honkansa, ja mennä hänen viimeiseen majaansa, hänen ihanan vainionsa metsään.
verses.king_james: By thy messengers thou hast reproached the Lord, and hast said, With the multitude of my chariots I am come up to the height of the mountains, to the sides of Lebanon, and will cut down the tall cedar trees thereof, and the choice fir trees thereof: and I will enter into the lodgings of his borders, and into the forest of his Carmel.

verses.latin_vulgate: ego succidi.  Et bibi aquas alienas, et siccavi vestigiis pedum meorum omnes aquas clausas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֣י קַ֔רְתִּי וְשָׁתִ֖יתִי מַ֣יִם זָרִ֑ים וְאַחְרִב֙ בְּכַף־פְּעָמַ֔י כֹּ֖ל יְאֹרֵ֥י מָצֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have cut down, and I have drunk strange waters, and have dried up with the soles of my feet all the shut up waters.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen kaivanut ja juonut muukalaiset vedet, ja olen kuivannut kaikki piiritetyt ojat minun jalkapöydälläni.
verses.king_james: I have digged and drunk strange waters, and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of besieged places.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid non audisti quid ab initio fecerim ?  ex diebus antiquis plasmavi illud, et nunc adduxi :  eruntque in ruinam collium pugnantium civitates munitæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹֽא־שָׁמַ֤עְתָּ לְמֵֽרָחוֹק֙ אֹתָ֣הּ עָשִׂ֔יתִי לְמִ֥ימֵי קֶ֖דֶם וִיצַרְתִּ֑יהָ עַתָּ֣ה הֲבֵיאתִ֔יהָ וּתְהִ֗י לַהְשׁ֛וֹת גַּלִּ֥ים נִצִּ֖ים עָרִ֥ים בְּצֻרֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hast thou not heard what I have done from the beginning? from the days of old I have formed it, and now I have brought it to effect: that fenced cities of fighting men should be turned to heaps of ruins:
verses.finnish_biblia: Etkös ole kaukaa kuullut minun näitä tehneeksi, ja jo aikaa ennen näitä valmistaneeksi? Nyt olen minä sen antanut tapahtua, että vahvat kaupungit pitäis kukistettaman hävinneeksi kiviroukkioksi,
verses.king_james: Hast thou not heard long ago how I have done it, and of ancient times that I have formed it? now have I brought it to pass, that thou shouldest be to lay waste fenced cities into ruinous heaps.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui sedent in eis, humiles manu, contremuerunt et confusi sunt :  facti sunt velut fœnum agri, et virens herba tectorum,  quæ arefacta est antequam veniret ad maturitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֹֽשְׁבֵיהֶן֙ קִצְרֵי־יָ֔ד חַ֖תּוּ וַיֵּבֹ֑שׁוּ הָי֞וּ עֵ֤שֶׂב שָׂדֶה֙ וִ֣ירַק דֶּ֔שֶׁא חֲצִ֣יר גַּגּ֔וֹת וּשְׁדֵפָ֖ה לִפְנֵ֥י קָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the inhabitants of them were weak of hand, they trembled and were confounded, they became like the grass of the field, and the green herb on the tops of houses, which withered before it came to maturity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ne, jotka siinä asuvat, pitäis tuleman heikoksi, peljästymän, häpeemän ja tuleman niinkuin ruoho kedolla, ja niinkuin viheriäinen ruohon korsi katon päällä, ja niinkuin kuivettu oras ennen kuin se oljeksi tulee.
verses.king_james: Therefore their inhabitants were of small power, they were dismayed and confounded; they were as the grass of the field, and as the green herb, as the grass on the housetops, and as corn blasted before it be grown up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habitaculum tuum, et egressum tuum, et introitum tuum, et viam tuam ego præscivi,  et furorem tuum contra me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשִׁבְתְּךָ֛ וְצֵאתְךָ֥ וּבֹאֲךָ֖ יָדָ֑עְתִּי וְאֵ֖ת הִֽתְרַגֶּזְךָ֥ אֵלָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy dwelling, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy way I knew before, and thy rage against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä tiedän sinun asumasias, sinun ulos- ja sisällekäymises, ja ettäs kiukuitset minua vastaan.
verses.king_james: But I know thy abode, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy rage against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Insanisti in me, et superbia tua ascendit in aures meas :  ponam itaque circulum in naribus tuis, et camum in labiis tuis,  et reducam te in viam per quam venisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַ֚עַן הִתְרַגֶּזְךָ֣ אֵלַ֔י וְשַׁאֲנַנְךָ֖ עָלָ֣ה בְאׇזְנָ֑י וְשַׂמְתִּ֨י חַחִ֜י בְּאַפֶּ֗ךָ וּמִתְגִּי֙ בִּשְׂפָתֶ֔יךָ וַהֲשִׁ֣בֹתִ֔יךָ בַּדֶּ֖רֶךְ אֲשֶׁר־בָּ֥אתָ בָּֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast been mad against me, and thy pride hath come up to my ears: therefore I will put a ring in thy nose, and a bit between thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ettäs kiukuitset minua vastaan ja sinun ylpeytes on tullut minun korvilleni, panen minä renkaani sinun sieraimiis ja suitseni suuhus, ja johdatan sinun sitä tietä myöten takaperin, jotas tulitkin.
verses.king_james: Because thy rage against me and thy tumult is come up into mine ears, therefore I will put my hook in thy nose, and my bridle in thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tibi autem, Ezechia, hoc erit signum :  comede hoc anno quæ repereris :  in secundo autem anno, quæ sponte nascuntur :  porro in tertio anno seminate et metite :  plantate vineas, et comedite fructum earum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְזֶה־לְּךָ֣ הָא֔וֹת אָכ֤וֹל הַשָּׁנָה֙ סָפִ֔יחַ וּבַשָּׁנָ֥ה הַשֵּׁנִ֖ית סָחִ֑ישׁ וּבַשָּׁנָ֣ה הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֗ית זִרְע֧וּ וְקִצְר֛וּ וְנִטְע֥וּ כְרָמִ֖ים וְאִכְל֥וּ פִרְיָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And to thee, O Ezechias, this shall be a sign: Eat this year what thou shalt find: and in the second year, such things as spring of themselves: but in the third year sow and reap: plant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tämä pitää oleman sinulle merkiksi: syö tällä ajastajalla sitä, mikä kasvanut on, toisella ajastajalla sitä, mikä itsestänsä kasvaa; mutta kolmannella ajastajalla kylväkäät ja leikatkaat, ja istuttakaat viinamäkiä, syökäät heidän hedelmänsä.
verses.king_james: And this shall be a sign unto thee, Ye shall eat this year such things as grow of themselves, and in the second year that which springeth of the same; and in the third year sow ye, and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat the fruits thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quodcumque reliquum fuerit de domo Juda, mittet radicem deorsum,  et faciet fructum sursum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָ֨סְפָ֜ה פְּלֵיטַ֧ת בֵּית־יְהוּדָ֛ה הַנִּשְׁאָרָ֖ה שֹׁ֣רֶשׁ לְמָ֑טָּה וְעָשָׂ֥ה פְרִ֖י לְמָֽעְלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And whatsoever shall be left of the house of Juda, shall take root downward, and bear fruit upward.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja joka Juudan huoneesta päässyt ja jäänyt on, pitää vielä nyt alta juurtuman ja ylhäältä hedelmöitsemän.
verses.king_james: And the remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah shall yet again take root downward, and bear fruit upward.

verses.latin_vulgate: De Jerusalem quippe egredientur reliquiæ,  et quod salvetur de monte Sion :  zelus Domini exercituum faciet hoc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י מִירוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ תֵּצֵ֣א שְׁאֵרִ֔ית וּפְלֵיטָ֖ה מֵהַ֣ר צִיּ֑וֹן קִנְאַ֛ת יְהֹוָ֥ה [צְבָא֖וֹת] תַּעֲשֶׂה־זֹּֽאת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and that which shall be saved out of mount Sion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä jääneet pitää käymän Jerusalemista ulos, ja Zionin vuorelta ne, jotka päässeet ovat. Herran Zebaotin kiivaus tekee näitä.
verses.king_james: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and they that escape out of mount Zion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam ob rem hæc dicit Dominus de rege Assyriorum :  Non ingredietur urbem hanc, nec mittet in eam sagittam,  nec occupabit eam clypeus, nec circumdabit eam munitio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן כֹּֽה־אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ אַשּׁ֔וּר לֹ֤א יָבֹא֙ אֶל־הָעִ֣יר הַזֹּ֔את וְלֹא־יוֹרֶ֥ה שָׁ֖ם חֵ֑ץ וְלֹא־יְקַדְּמֶ֣נָּה מָגֵ֔ן וְלֹא־יִשְׁפֹּ֥ךְ עָלֶ֖יהָ סֹלְלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of the Assyrians: He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow into it, nor come before it with shield, nor cast a trench about it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden sanoo Herra Assyrian kuninkaasta näin: ei hänen pidä tuleman tähän kaupunkiin, eikä yhtään nuolta sinne ampuman, ei myös yhtään kilpeä tuoman sen eteen, ja ei yhtään vallia rakentaman sen ympärille;
verses.king_james: Therefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come before it with shield, nor cast a bank against it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per viam qua venit, revertetur :  et civitatem hanc non ingredietur, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּדֶּ֥רֶךְ אֲשֶׁר־יָבֹ֖א בָּ֣הּ יָשׁ֑וּב וְאֶל־הָעִ֥יר הַזֹּ֛את לֹ֥א יָבֹ֖א נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: By the way that he came he shall return: and into this city he shall not come, saith the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hänen pitää sitä tietä palajaman, jota hän tullut on, ja ei pidä hänen tähän kaupunkiin tuleman, sanoo Herra.
verses.king_james: By the way that he came, by the same shall he return, and shall not come into this city, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Protegamque urbem hanc, et salvabo eam propter me,  et propter David servum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַנּוֹתִ֛י אֶל־הָעִ֥יר הַזֹּ֖את לְהוֹשִׁיעָ֑הּ לְמַֽעֲנִ֔י וּלְמַ֖עַן דָּוִ֥ד עַבְדִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will protect this city, and will save it for my own sake, and for David, my servant's sake.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä varjelen tämän kaupungin ja pelastan sen minun tähteni ja Davidin minun palveliani tähden.
verses.king_james: For I will defend this city, to save it, for mine own sake, and for my servant David’s sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est igitur in nocte illa, venit angelus Domini, et percussit in castris Assyriorum centum octoginta quinque millia. Cumque diluculo surrexisset, vidit omnia corpora mortuorum : et recedens abiit,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֮ בַּלַּ֣יְלָה הַהוּא֒ וַיֵּצֵ֣א ׀ מַלְאַ֣ךְ יְהֹוָ֗ה וַיַּךְ֙ בְּמַחֲנֵ֣ה אַשּׁ֔וּר מֵאָ֛ה שְׁמוֹנִ֥ים וַחֲמִשָּׁ֖ה אָ֑לֶף וַיַּשְׁכִּ֣ימוּ בַבֹּ֔קֶר וְהִנֵּ֥ה כֻלָּ֖ם פְּגָרִ֥ים מֵתִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass that night, that an angel of the Lord came, and slew in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred and eighty-five thousand. And when he arose early in the morning, he saw all the bodies of the dead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tapahtui sinä yönä, että Herran enkeli meni ja tappoi Assyrian leirissä sata kahdeksankymmentä ja viisituhatta miestä. Ja kuin he silloin varhain huomeneltain nousivat, katso, ne kaikki olivat kuolleitten ruumiit.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass that night, that the angel of the Lord went out, and smote in the camp of the Assyrians an hundred fourscore and five thousand: and when they arose early in the morning, behold, they were all dead corpses.

verses.latin_vulgate: et reversus est Sennacherib rex Assyriorum, et mansit in Ninive.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּסַּ֣ע וַיֵּ֔לֶךְ וַיָּ֖שׇׁב סַנְחֵרִ֣יב מֶלֶךְ־אַשּׁ֑וּר וַיֵּ֖שֶׁב בְּנִֽינְוֵֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Sennacherib, king of the Assyrians, departing, went away, and he returned and abode in Ninive.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Sanherib Assyrian kuningas meni matkaansa, palasi ja oli Ninivessä.
verses.king_james: So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed, and went and returned, and dwelt at Nineveh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque adoraret in templo Nesroch deum suum, Adramelech et Sarasar filii ejus percusserunt eum gladio, fugeruntque in terram Armeniorum : et regnavit Asarhaddon filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֩ ה֨וּא מִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֜ה בֵּ֣ית ׀ נִסְרֹ֣ךְ אֱלֹהָ֗יו וְֽאַדְרַמֶּ֨לֶךְ וְשַׂרְאֶ֤צֶר [בָּנָיו֙] הִכֻּ֣הוּ בַחֶ֔רֶב וְהֵ֥מָּה נִמְלְט֖וּ אֶ֣רֶץ אֲרָרָ֑ט וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ אֵסַר־חַדֹּ֥ן בְּנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And as he was worshipping in the temple of Nesroch, his god, Adramelech and Sarasar, his sons, slew him with the sword, and they fled into the land of the Armenians, and Asarhaddon, his son, reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän rukoili Nisrokin jumalansa huoneessa, niin hänen poikansa Adramelek ja Saretser tappoivat hänen miekalla ja pakenivat Araratin maakuntaan: ja hänen poikansa Assarhaddon tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as he was worshipping in the house of Nisroch his god, that Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons smote him with the sword: and they escaped into the land of Armenia. And Esarhaddon his son reigned in his stead.
